sector: Industrials — Building Products & Equipment (commercial HVAC, controls, fire & security, data-center thermal)
bull_thesis: JCI is a transformed, purified mission-critical buildings franchise riding the AI-thermal wave with a credible new margin engine. Three legs hold the case: (1) DEMAND IS REAL AND ACCELERATING — Q2 FY26 orders +30% organic, backlog a record $20.0B (+26% organic), up from $18B/+20% in Q1, explicitly DC-led (new YDAM/YKHT chiller platforms + an NVIDIA thermal reference-architecture collaboration positioning JCI inside hyperscaler/sovereign-AI campus specs). Management frames it as 'architecting the thermal backbone for the next generation of AI computing.' (2) MARGIN IS INFLECTING STRUCTURALLY — adj EBIT margin 15.5% (+310bp YoY) in Q2 vs 12.4% (+190bp) in Q1, expanding across ALL three regions (Americas 19.5%, EMEA 14.9% +370bp, APAC 19.8% +350bp). This is the ex-Danaher CEO Weidemanis operating system (simplify/accelerate/amplify — 80/20, lean, AI) compounding, not a one-off; adj EPS +45% YoY. (3) CAPITAL DISCIPLINE ARRIVED — the $8.1B Bosch R&LC HVAC sale (closed Aug-2025, ~$5.0B net) funded a $5.0B executed ASR; net debt/EBITDA down to 2.0x (from 2.4x); ~100% FCF conversion; FY26 guide RAISED to ~$4.85 adj EPS / ~6% organic / ~50% operating leverage. The moat is the sticky installed base + service attach + OpenBlue controls + designed-in DC specs — high-margin recurring revenue that compounds as the new DC backlog converts. If the backlog converts (70% expected within 12 months) and margins march into the high-teens, EPS power and the multiple both support $165-185. JCI is on the WINNING side of AI build-out AND electrification/decarbonization retrofit, with a diversified mission-critical demand base (DC + life sciences + pharma + research campuses) that is more durable than pure AI-capex beta.
bear_thesis: The good news is in the price, and the load-bearing catalyst is a forward option on the same shared macro factor — hyperscaler AI-capex — that the entire scale-out book already depends on. Four problems: (1) THE ORDERS/REVENUE GAP — orders +30% and backlog +26%, but FY26 organic revenue guidance is only ~6%. Management ITSELF flags backlog is 'longer-cycle,' conversion 'depends on customer availability and ability to accept orders,' and faster revenue 'won't come at operating leverage in the fifties.' The explosive AI demand is NOT current revenue; it is a deferred, customer-controlled option. (2) VALUATION HAS NO MARGIN OF SAFETY — at ~24.75x forward P/E and ~27x EV/EBITDA on a +6% organic grower, near all-time highs ($147.75 on Jun 9, 2026), the stock already discounts successful conversion AND continued margin expansion. EV/EBITDA at 27x is RICHER than Trane (~24.5x) and far above Carrier (~18x) despite slower organic growth than either's DC exposure. (3) PRICING POWER IS WEAKER ON THE MARQUEE WINS — management conceded 'competitive bidding on larger orders' and pivoted language from 'pricing' to 'value-based engineering' — i.e., the headline hyperscaler DC equipment orders compete on price against Trane/Carrier/Vertiv and may dilute incremental margin. (4) CATALYST IS ALREADY-FIRED / ALREADY-RAN — the order/backlog inflection, the Bosch ASR, and the margin re-rate have ALL happened and are reflected; the stock has run hard into the AI-thermal narrative. A hyperscaler capex digestion (the regime's #1 tripwire — AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) would slow conversion and de-rate a high-multiple, mid-single-digit grower sharply. Downside to a fair ~22x on conservative EPS is mid-$120s/$115.
sop_bull: $172-185/share. Whole-company intrinsic (post-Bosch the three geographic segments are an integrated buildings franchise, so value the whole on the right multiple rather than break apart). Bull EPS power: FY27 adj EPS ~$5.50 as backlog converts and margins reach ~16.5-17% (operating leverage on the $20B backlog). Apply a premium ~31-33x (toward Trane's quality multiple, justified by accelerating orders + clean balance sheet + improving ROIC) → ~$170-182. Overlay: net debt only 2.0x leaves buyback firepower + ~$5B ASR already shrinking the share count, accreting another ~$5-8/share. SoP-by-region cross-check: Americas (~$4.1B/qtr, 19.5% EBITA, the DC + service core) is the crown jewel and would command a premium standalone; EMEA inflecting (+370bp); APAC fastest-growing (+16%). Sum supports $172-185.
sop_bear: $112-122/share. Conservative whole-company: assume backlog converts slowly and AI-capex digests — FY27 adj EPS holds ~$4.85-5.00 (no acceleration), margins plateau ~15.5%. De-rate to a cyclical-industrial ~22-24x (toward Carrier's ~18x EV/EBITDA / fair-PE band, reflecting that the DC equipment piece is price-competitive and the grower is only ~6% organic) → ~$110-120. Net debt 2.0x is benign (no balance-sheet risk), and the $5B ASR provides a floor, but offers no rescue if the multiple compresses. SoP-by-region cross-check: strip the AI-thermal premium and value the integrated franchise as a mid-single-digit commercial-buildings compounder → mid-teens EV/EBITDA on ~$5B EBITDA → ~$112-122.
sop_breakdown: Base case = whole-company intrinsic on the integrated post-Bosch franchise. FY27 adj EPS ~$5.20 (steady ~6-7% organic + ~50% operating leverage + ASR share-count shrink) at ~27-28x (a justified premium to Carrier's ~23.6x fwd, a modest discount to Trane's ~29-30x, reflecting JCI's improving-but-still-narrower moat and slower organic growth than Trane). That yields ~$140-145. Cross-checks: (a) EV/EBITDA — ~$5.0-5.3B EBITDA at ~25-26x (between Carrier 18x and Trane 24.5x, edging above Trane on order momentum) ≈ EV ~$130B less ~$9B net debt ≈ ~$148/sh; (b) the $5B executed ASR + 2.0x leverage adds a few dollars of floor. Parts (regional, post-Bosch): Americas (the DC/service crown jewel, 19.5% EBITA) is the majority of value and would command a premium standalone; EMEA (inflecting, 14.9% +370bp) and APAC (fastest, 19.8% +350bp) add the growth. Net base ~$145, essentially AT the ~$147 market price — the AI-thermal optionality is substantially priced in.
risk_reward: Poor/symmetric at ~$147. Upside-to-fair ~ -1% to +2% (fair ~$145, base); upside-to-bull ~+17-26% ($172-185) requires backlog conversion + margins to high-teens; downside-to-bear ~ -17% to -24% ($112-122) on an AI-capex digestion. Roughly 1:1 risk/reward at the current price — the asymmetry is gone because the stock ran into the catalyst. Better entry on a hyperscaler-capex flush toward the mid-$120s would restore 2:1.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED / partially FIRED. The load-bearing catalysts have largely ALREADY FIRED and are reflected: (1) the orders/backlog inflection — +30% orders, $20.0B backlog (+26%) — is a realized, dated print (Q2 FY26 release May 6, 2026), not a forward event; the stock is at/near all-time highs ($147.75 Jun 9, 2026) having already run it. (2) The Bosch $8.1B R&LC divestiture CLOSED Aug 1, 2025 and the $5.0B ASR is EXECUTED — fully FIRED. (3) The FY26 guidance raise (to ~$4.85) is done. What remains LIVE is the SOFT, customer-controlled, serially-deferred BACKLOG-CONVERSION catalyst — '70% of $20B converts within 12 months,' but management explicitly conditions it on hyperscaler 'customer availability and ability to accept orders,' i.e., a non-binding, third-party-dependent, single-shared-factor (AI-capex) driver. There is NO hard-dated binding catalyst (no PDUFA, no NRC milestone, no fixed deal terms). Per the regime doc, an already-fired/already-ran catalyst on the same shared AI-capex factor as the rest of the basket is PENALIZED.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers within the scale-out THERMAL layer (live-verified mid-June 2026): Trane Technologies (TT) — fwd P/E ~29-30x, EV/EBITDA ~24.5x; the highest-quality DC-thermal pure-play, fastest-growing, richest multiple. Carrier Global (CARR) — fwd P/E ~23.6x, EV/EBITDA ~18.1x; cheapest, post-portfolio-churn, lower DC mix. Vertiv (VRT) — the highest AI-beta thermal+power name (liquid cooling / CDU / busway), trades at a large growth premium (~30-35x+ fwd) on much faster growth — the purest AI-capex play in the layer. JCI itself — fwd P/E ~24.75x, EV/EBITDA ~27x, ~6% organic, adj EBIT margin 15.5% rising. RANKING: VALUATION — JCI mid-pack on P/E (above CARR, below TT) but RICHEST on EV/EBITDA (~27x > TT 24.5x > CARR 18x), which is a stretch given it is the SLOWEST organic grower of the cohort. GROWTH — JCI's ORDER growth (+30%) is best-in-class, but converted REVENUE (~6% organic) trails TT and VRT; VRT >> TT > JCI ~ CARR on near-term top line. MARGINS — improving fastest (+310bp) off a lower base; TT structurally highest, JCI now closing the gap, CARR mid. RETURNS — JCI ROIC historically mediocre (~7-8%) but rising ~3-4pts/yr; TT best-in-class ROIC; JCI re-rating its returns profile. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — VRT highest (power+cooling), then TT and JCI (thermal/chillers/controls), CARR lower. NET: JCI is a quality-improving #3 in the cohort trading at a full multiple — its EV/EBITDA premium to TT is unjustified on growth and only defensible on the order-momentum + margin-inflection narrative.
role_in_scaleout: JCI supplies the data-center THERMAL backbone — large-scale chillers (YDAM/YKHT), CRAH/air handling, controls (OpenBlue) and integrated build-out for hyperscaler/sovereign-AI campuses, sitting inside NVIDIA thermal reference architectures; demand is durable (cooling is non-optional for every AI campus and the service/controls attach is sticky for the asset life) but the marquee equipment orders are price-competitive vs Trane/Carrier/Vertiv, so JCI captures the recurring-service moat more than premium equipment pricing.
consensus_delta: There is little exploitable consensus delta LEFT — the street has already caught up to the AI-thermal story, which is precisely the problem. Wall Street is NOT looking the wrong way; consensus has bid JCI to ~24.75x forward / ~27x EV/EBITDA at all-time highs ($147.75 Jun 9, 2026) explicitly FOR the data-center backlog. The thesis is consensus, not contrarian. The only genuine analytical wedge is the orders-to-revenue conversion bridge: bulls extrapolate +30% orders into revenue acceleration, while management ITSELF guides only ~6% organic and warns conversion is customer-controlled and long-cycle ('a lot depends on customer availability and ability to accept orders'). The street's error, if any, is OVER-discounting near-term conversion — pricing the $20B backlog as if it converts on JCI's timetable when it converts on the hyperscalers'. That is a downside risk to consensus, not an upside arb. There is no hidden-good-news mispricing here; the expectations are full.
valley_of_death: No balance-sheet valley — net debt is a benign 2.0x, FCF conversion ~100%, no maturity wall, the Bosch cash is in and the ASR is executed. The real 3-9 month risk is a MULTIPLE valley, not a cash valley: (1) the regime's #1 tripwire is an AI-capex break (hyperscaler capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth); any hyperscaler capex guide-down or DC-digestion scare would slow JCI's backlog conversion AND de-rate a 27x EV/EBITDA name simultaneously — a double hit toward the mid-$120s. (2) The orders/revenue gap means a quarter where orders DECELERATE from the +30% pace (a tough comp setup) could puncture the narrative even with strong margins. (3) Pricing concession on large DC orders ('competitive bidding') could show up as softer incremental margins on the marquee wins. (4) A complacent, fully-invested tape (BofA FMS cash 3.9%, Mag-7 ~30% of index) has little dry powder to absorb an AI-capex shock, so a de-gross would hit the AI-adjacent industrials hard and indiscriminately.
positioning_washout: No dividend-cut or pivot-driven forced selling — JCI pays a stable, well-covered dividend ($244M/qtr) and the shareholder base is quality-industrial/index, not yield-trapped. The mechanical dynamic runs the OTHER way: the executed $5.0B ASR is a steady, price-insensitive BUYER shrinking the float, which supports the stock and removes a washout setup. That is a negative for an aggressive entry — there is no forced-seller flush to buy into; the stock is well-owned and well-bid. A washout would require an exogenous AI-capex shock to de-gross the crowded AI-adjacent industrials cohort; absent that, there is no mechanical capitulation to exploit.
forcing_function: The recurring quarterly earnings prints are the only forcing events, and they cut both ways — the next FY26 quarter (Q3, ~early Aug 2026) is the dated window where the market re-tests the orders/revenue conversion thesis: a print showing the $20B backlog beginning to inflect REVENUE (not just orders) would force an upgrade, while orders decelerating off the +30% comp or a guide that holds organic at ~6% despite the backlog would puncture the AI-conversion narrative. There is NO hard binding corporate event (no spin, no deal close pending, no NRC/PPA/PDUFA) — the re-rate is gated entirely on hyperscaler capex behavior showing up in JCI's conversion, which is exogenous and not management-controlled.
moderator_conclusion: PASS (C), conviction 2. JCI is a genuinely STRENGTHENING, high-quality franchise — the Interrogator's read (credibility 4, narrow-but-widening moat, manageable secular threat) is correct and corroborated: real accelerating orders (+30%), a clean de-levered balance sheet (2.0x), a credible ex-Danaher margin engine compounding (+310bp), and shareholder returns funded by the completed Bosch divestiture. As a BUSINESS this is one of the better names in the scale-out book. But as an ENTRY at ~$147, the case fails the regime test on three counts: (1) the load-bearing catalyst is largely ALREADY-FIRED / ALREADY-RAN — the order inflection, the Bosch ASR, the guidance raise have all happened and the stock is at all-time highs having run them; the regime doc explicitly PENALIZES already-fired catalysts. (2) The remaining live catalyst (backlog conversion) is SOFT, customer-controlled, serially-deferred, and hinges on the SINGLE shared AI-capex factor that the ENTIRE basket already depends on — exactly the resolution-driver-independence violation §1.7 says to enforce against; adding JCI concentrates the book in the same AI-capex tail. (3) Valuation has no margin of safety — ~27x EV/EBITDA is RICHER than Trane (24.5x) despite slower organic growth, and the base-case SoP (~$145) sits essentially AT the market price, with ~1:1 risk/reward. The AI-thermal optionality is substantially priced in. This is a WATCH, not a BUY: the right move is to wait for an AI-capex flush toward the mid-$120s (where risk/reward returns to ~2:1 and the franchise quality is bought at a discount), not to chase it at all-time highs into the most-crowded factor in the tape. VALUE_CONVICTION 3 (not lower): stripping the catalyst/regime overlay, the franchise quality, improving returns, clean balance sheet and ~100% FCF conversion are real and the SoP says fairly-valued (not expensive-on-a-melting-base, not a trap) — but at full price with no discount to intrinsic, the standalone value case is only fair, capped at 3 by the absence of a margin of safety.
interrogator_dossier:
# INTERROGATOR FORENSIC DOSSIER — Johnson Controls International (JCI)

*Scale-out Basket C (THERMAL). Lens: the AI compute build-out's physical bottlenecks — here, data-center cooling/thermal management + the controls and build-out systems every hyperscaler campus must install. Prepared as Claude Opus 4.8, allocating real capital. Sources: Q2 FY2026 release (May 6, 2026), Q1 FY2026 transcript (Feb 4, 2026), FY25 results, Bosch R&LC HVAC divestiture (closed Aug 1, 2025). Price ~$147, mkt cap ~$87-90B as of mid-June 2026.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

JCI's story has undergone a deliberate, multi-year metamorphosis from a sprawling, conglomerate-flavored buildings-and-batteries holding company into a focused pure-play commercial building solutions franchise, and the AI-data-center thermal wave has landed on top of that simplification at exactly the right moment. The arc has three legs: (1) Portfolio purification — culminating in the Aug-1-2025 close of the $8.1B sale of the Residential & Light Commercial HVAC business to Bosch (~$5.0B net cash), which removed the lower-margin consumer-cyclical business and recast JCI as mission-critical commercial buildings + controls + service, re-segmented into Americas/EMEA/APAC. (2) Management reset — new CEO Joakim Weidemanis (ex-Danaher) brought the Danaher Business System (simplify/accelerate/amplify), shifting the narrative from portfolio strategy to execution and self-help margin. (3) AI-thermal overlay — 'architecting the thermal backbone for the next generation of AI computing,' an NVIDIA thermal-reference-architecture collaboration, new YDAM/YKHT chillers; management consistently diversifies the story (DC + life sciences + pharma + research campuses), a tell they want durability not just AI beta. The story expanded and sharpened, not drifted.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Demand 'AI/DC-led' — CORROBORATED: orders +30% organic, backlog $20.0B (+26%), accelerating from $18B/+20% in Q1. Margin 'structural' — CORROBORATED and ACCELERATING: adj EBIT 15.5% (+310bp) vs Q1 12.4% (+190bp), expanding all three regions (Americas 19.5%, EMEA 14.9% +370bp, APAC 19.8% +350bp). '~100% FCF conversion' — CORROBORATED (Q2 FCF $604M, adj $526M). 'De-levered' — CORROBORATED: 2.0x vs 2.4x yr-ago. 'Returning capital' — CORROBORATED: $5.0B executed ASR. 'Backlog converts' — TENSION: 70% of $20B within 12mo claimed, yet FY26 organic guide only ~6%; management flags backlog is long-cycle and conversion 'depends on customer availability and ability to accept orders.' The orders(+30%)/revenue(+6%) gap is the forensic crux — honest physics of long-cycle DC construction, but it caps near-term earnings flow-through.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising and more specific. FY26 guidance RAISED twice (to ~$4.85 adj EPS / ~6% organic / ~50% leverage); adj EPS +45% YoY in Q2 is a put-up print. Firmly offensive language ('thermal backbone') with disciplined caveats (China won't return to prior growth; backlog long-cycle). Under-promising on revenue timing, over-delivering on margin — the inverse of a hype cycle.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

High. Pattern within FY26 is raise-and-beat; the deliberate refusal to guide revenue up to match +30% orders (holding ~6%) is sandbagging-with-integrity. Caveat: the new-CEO cadence is only a few quarters old. Reliability: High.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

The orders-to-revenue conversion bridge is the recurring pressure point — the re-rate to revenue acceleration is deferred and customer-controlled (hinges on hyperscaler campus readiness). Secondary: pricing on large orders ('competitive bidding on larger orders'), China/APAC durability, capacity to scale to the backlog. None turned into bad news yet; conversion-timing is the one most likely to disappoint if hyperscaler capex slows.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: orders +30%/backlog +26% two quarters running; margin inflection across all regions (+310bp); clean balance sheet (2.0x) + $5B executed ASR + raised guidance; purified mission-critical portfolio; credible Danaher-style operating system. RED: orders/revenue gap (+30% vs +6% — AI demand is a forward option not current cash); competitive bidding on large DC orders (weaker pricing power on marquee wins); multiple already discounts the good news (~25x fwd / ~27x EV/EBITDA at all-time highs); adj FCF < reported FCF (watch the bridge).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Deliberate diversification of the AI narrative (always pairs DC with life sciences/pharma/research) — managing AI-concentration risk, selling durability. 'Value-based engineering' replacing 'pricing' — competing on TCO/embedded controls/service, conceding marquee DC equipment is price-competitive. NVIDIA reference-architecture collaboration = a designed-in spec foothold. New voices = Danaher DNA.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT

STRENGTHENING — genuinely improving franchise (accelerating orders, clean balance sheet, credible operating system, purified portfolio, returns funded by divestiture). The single thing to weigh before sizing: the stock already prices the good news — ~25x fwd / ~27x EV/EBITDA on a ~6% organic grower bakes in successful conversion AND continued margin expansion; the +30% orders are a forward option on hyperscaler capex durability (the SAME factor the rest of the book depends on), so asymmetry is poor at this price. Moat: NARROW, modestly WIDENING — switching costs/intangibles via installed base + service attach + OpenBlue controls + designed-in specs (rising ROIC, +310bp margin argue widening), but marquee DC equipment competes head-to-head on price (narrow there). Secular threat: MANAGEABLE — no displacement/disintermediation; JCI is on the winning side of AI build-out + electrification; the real risk is cyclical (hyperscaler capex digestion), not terminal. Not a cheap fear-print — a fairly-to-fully-valued quality compounder whose AI optionality is largely priced.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: No clean kill: every load-bearing figure verifies against the primary Q2 FY26 release (SEC 8-K / PR Newswire, May 6 2026) and the Bosch 8-K (Aug 1 2025). The bear case (already-fired catalyst + no margin of safety) is itself CONFIRMED, not refuted — but it argues 'fairly-valued, poor entry,' not a broken thesis. The one factual defect: the dossier/bull's 'Americas +310bp' is wrong — Americas adj segment EBITA margin rose only +100bp YoY (19.5%); the +310bp figure is the TOTAL adj segment margin to 15.5%, not Americas. EMEA +370bp / APAC +350bp verify. This does not break the case but the per-region margin-inflection claim is overstated for Americas (the 'crown jewel').
skeptic_corrections: (1) Americas margin expansion is +100bp YoY (not +310bp); +310bp is the consolidated adj segment EBITA margin to 15.5%. (2) The '70% of backlog converts within 12 months' and the 'NVIDIA thermal reference-architecture collaboration' claims are NOT in the official earnings release — they trace to transcript/IR commentary, not the primary press release; treat as management-asserted, not press-release-verified. (3) Bosch net proceeds ~$5.0B after tax verify, but the headline $8.1B is the TOTAL deal; JCI's gross consideration was ~$6.7B — use the net (~$5.0B) for the ASR-funding math. (4) Current price ~$146 (Jun 2026), ATH $147.75 on Jun 9 2026 verifies; the SoP base ~$145 sits AT market — risk/reward ~1:1, catalyst already fired. Cap conviction accordingly.
skeptic_evidence: SEC Form 8-K / PR Newswire, 'Johnson Controls Reports Strong Q2 Results; Raises FY26 Guidance,' May 6 2026 — Q2 sales $6.1B, organic +6%, orders +30% organic, backlog $20.0B (+26% organic; Americas $14.9B / EMEA $3.2B / APAC $1.9B), adj EPS $1.19 (+45%), adj segment EBITA margin 15.5%, Americas +100bp/EMEA +370bp/APAC +350bp, net debt 2.0x, ~100% FCF conversion, FY26 guide raised to ~$4.85 adj EPS / ~6% organic / ~50% leverage.

Johnson Controls press release / SEC 8-K, 'Completes Sale of Residential and Light Commercial HVAC Business,' Aug 1 2025 — $8.1B total deal value, JCI consideration ~$6.7B, ~$5.0B net cash after tax, $5.0B accelerated share repurchase program announced.

Macrotrends / stockanalysis.com price history (accessed Jun 2026) — JCI all-time-high close $147.75 on Jun 9 2026; price ~$146, mkt cap ~$89B; forward P/E ~25-27x, EV/EBITDA ~22-28x (source-dependent) — full multiple at all-time highs, confirming the 'priced-in / no margin of safety' bear.

Q2 FY26 release reconciliation — orders +30% organic vs FY26 organic revenue guide only ~6%: the orders-to-revenue conversion gap is real and management-acknowledged, confirming the catalyst is a deferred, customer-controlled (hyperscaler-capex-gated) option, not current revenue.

scale.role: Building/HVAC controls with a DC backlog leg; orders +30%, backlog $20.0B (+26%).
scale.rationale: Quality re-rate (orders +30% organic, backlog $20.0B) but the DC leg is a minority of a broad buildings business — a TACTICAL-to-low-LEVER franchise. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a material per-region fix (Americas margin expanded +100bp not +310bp; the NVIDIA/thermal collaboration and 70%-conversion claims are transcript color, not in the release), so a ~10pt haircut off a ~54 franchise. Catalyst SOFT_EXTENDED/fired into all-time highs, base ~$145 ~at spot (~1:1); wait.
scale.demand_durability: Medium — building controls/HVAC, DC a growing but minority slice.
